title: IMD issued orange alert in six districts of Kerala - News On AIR
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Kerala
full_text: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert in six districts of Kerala today, indicating isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall. An orange alert has been issued in Pathanamthitta, Kottayam, Idukki, Malappuram, Kozhikode, and Wayanad districts. The Weather Department has asked fishermen not to venture into the sea, as squally weather with windspeed reaching up to 55 kmph is likely to prevail along and off the coast of Kerala and Lakshadweep. Meanwhile, the district authorities in Pathanamthitta said water from Muzhiyar dam and Maniyar barrage is likely to be released to adjust the water level following heavy rain in the catchment areas. Those living along the banks of Kakkattar and Pampa have been asked to remain alert.
